name: Fujiyama Texas
tagline: The ultimate late-night salvation — kushi katsu (deep-fried skewers) until 3:30am, perfect for when the clubs close and you need real food.
parking: Small lot behind restaurant off S. King St; street parking on side streets in Moiliili
talkStory: A Moiliili institution since the 1980s, Fujiyama Texas was founded by a Japanese chef who fell in love with Texas barbecue — the name itself captures Hawaii's cultural mash-up spirit. The kushi katsu (deep-fried skewers dipped in house sauce) are the real draw, served until 3:30am most nights (closed Tue). The late-night 'Midnight Diner' menu kicks in at 11pm and draws a loyal crowd of service industry workers, insomniacs, and post-bar revelers seeking real food.
